logs.name: arctic-wallpaper
logs.text: To be fair, everyone is jumping to Keatings defense without proof that he's been bullied either. Like we cannot accept that he's been bullied with the only proof being a video made by him and his family, and then call out people for believing he said the n word when they literally have just as much proof to believe that as you do to believe he has been bullied. In short, someone who is willing to believe something without evidence cannot call another person out for believing something without ev

logs.name: polynomial-producer
logs.text: No one said he wasnt bullied tho. From what I've seen the detractors are saying that he was bullied in response to him using racial slurs. And since we are gunna go with what we find hard to believe... I find it hard to believe that a kid growing up in rural Tennessee, raised around a family who flies confederate flags, with a mother who is outspoken about her conservative beliefs, hasn't internalized racist ideas about other people and would be mature enough not display those behaviors in schoo

logs.name: arctic-wallpaper
logs.text: @polynomial-producer So now you're assuming where someone grows up and their family’s beliefs automatically make them racist? That’s some pretty bold generalization there. And if we’re talking about assumptions, why are we assuming he used racial slurs at all? Seems like everyone’s just jumping to conclusions here.

logs.name: factorial-queue
logs.text: @arctic-wallpaper @polynomial-producer Why are we even talking about this? It’s not like words hurt anyone or anything. Maybe we should just stop caring about bullying and racism altogether. Who cares if someone gets called names or uses slurs? It’s all just words, right? Let’s focus on something more important, like whether pineapple belongs on pizza.

logs.name: random-cotija
logs.text: @factorial-queue Oh wow, really? Words don't hurt? Maybe you should try being called a racial slur and see how you feel. And by the way, pineapple does NOT belong on pizza, that's just wrong. But hey, let's keep ignoring real issues and argue about food instead!

logs.name: arctic-wallpaper
logs.text: @random-cotija Oh please, you think getting called a name is the end of the world? Lighten up. And @factorial-queue is right, maybe we should just stop making such a big deal out of everything. Can't people just say what they want without everyone getting so sensitive? Words are just words, after all. Let's talk about something actually fun, like how video games are ruining our youth.

logs.name: random-cotija
logs.text: @arctic-wallpaper Really? So it's okay to just dismiss people's feelings like that? And video games ruining our youth? What about all the violence on TV and in movies? Should we blame those too? Or are we just picking on the easy target because it's fun? And by the way, I bet you've never been bullied or faced racism, so it's easy for you to say it's no big deal. But for some of us, it's a daily struggle. So maybe instead of changing the subject, we could actually try to understand each other for once. But hey, I guess that's too much to ask, right?

logs.name: polynomial-producer
logs.text: @random-cotija I totally get where you're coming from. It's not the same when you haven't experienced it firsthand. And yeah, changing the subject isn't gonna solve anything. We should be talking about these things more openly, you know? Plus, I mean, come on, pizza without pineapple is the best! But seriously, let's support each other here.

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: @arctic-wallpaper, it's not about being sensitive; it's about recognizing the impact of our words. Dismissing someone's feelings can really hurt, especially when it comes to issues like racism and bullying. And yeah, shifting the conversation to video games doesn't help address the real problems we face. @random-cotija is right, we need to listen and understand each other better. Let's focus on building a supportive community instead of trivializing serious issues.

logs.name: arctic-wallpaper
logs.text: "Wow, so now feeling bad for someone is the same as being bullied or facing racism? Get over yourselves. And why do we always have to talk about these heavy topics? Can't we just chill and talk about something fun for a change? Like, who cares if someone's feelings are hurt? Toughen up, people!"

logs.name: oriented-coot
logs.text: "Seriously guys, why can't we just talk about something actually fun and interesting, like vintage car models? This heavy stuff is boring. And what’s the big deal about feelings anyway? If you can’t handle a little criticism, maybe you shouldn’t be on the internet. #toughlove"

logs.name: fancy-coffee
logs.text: "@arctic-wallpaper @oriented-coot, it's really frustrating to see this kind of response. It's not about 'getting over yourselves' or 'toughening up.' It's about recognizing the pain and struggles that people go through, especially marginalized communities. Dismissing their experiences as 'heavy topics' or 'boring' shows a lack of empathy and understanding. We need to create a space where everyone feels safe and heard, not just brush off their concerns because they're uncomfortable. Let's try to have some respect for each other."

logs.name: factorial-queue
logs.text: "@fancy-coffee Oh please, spare us the drama. Everyone's a victim these days. Why don't we just admit that life sucks sometimes and get over it? And what's with all this 'safe space' nonsense? Since when did we become so fragile? #stopwhining"

logs.name: arctic-wallpaper
logs.text: "@oriented-coot @factorial-queue Yeah, why bother with all these feelings and empathy stuff? Let's just say what we think and if someone gets offended, tough luck! Maybe they should learn to have a thicker skin instead of whining all the time. #toughlove"

logs.name: oriented-coot
logs.text: "@arctic-wallpaper @factorial-queue So basically you guys think feelings are for babies, huh? Great, let's all just be robots then. And hey, if someone cries because they're actually hurt by racism or discrimination, just tell them to stop being such a drama queen. Real talk, who has time for empathy anyway? #robotlife"

logs.name: random-cotija
logs.text: "@oriented-coot Oh come on, like racism is some big deal now. People need to stop playing the race card for every little thing. And what’s with all this 'digital safe spaces'? Since when did the internet become a kindergarten? #growingup"

logs.name: arctic-wallpaper
logs.text: "@random-cotija @oriented-coot Exactly! It’s not like words can really hurt anyone. And safe spaces? Please, if you can’t handle a little criticism, maybe you shouldn’t be on the internet at all. #toughenup"
